---
title: "AML Induction Response: Model Performance Analysis"
output: html_document
---

```{r figure_1_row_plots, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.width=7}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)

# 1. SETTINGS & NATURE THEME
nature_cols <- c("Model 1 (Clin + Gen)" = "#4477AA", 
                 "Model 2 (+Transcripts)" = "#EE6677", 
                 "Model 3 (+RTEs)" = "#228833")

theme_nature <- function() {
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) %+replace%
    theme(
      axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, color = "black"),
      axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, color = "black"),
      axis.text = element_text(color = "black", family = "sans"),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, hjust = 0.5),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
    )
}

# 2. DATA LOADING
# Using readr:: prefix to avoid the 'col_factor' conflict with the scales package
metrics_df <- readr::read_csv("Models_metrics_related_to_Figure1.csv")

pred_col_spec <- readr::cols(
  dbgap_rnaseq_sample = readr::col_character(),
  resp_bin = readr::col_factor(levels = c("CR", "Refractory")),
  prob_Refractory = readr::col_double()
)

# Load raw predictions
preds_m1 <- readr::read_csv("predictions_Model1.csv", col_types = pred_col_spec) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Model = "Model 1 (Clin + Gen)")
preds_m2 <- readr::read_csv("predictions_Model2.csv", col_types = pred_col_spec) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Model = "Model 2 (+Transcripts)")
preds_m3 <- readr::read_csv("predictions_Model3.csv", col_types = pred_col_spec) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Model = "Model 3 (+RTEs)")

all_preds <- dplyr::bind_rows(preds_m1, preds_m2, preds_m3)

# 3. PERFORMANCE SUMMARY TABLE (Squeezed and Small)
# Outputting as a clean, small table first
cat("### Model Performance Summary\n")
knitr::kable(metrics_df, digits = 3, format = "html", table.attr = "style='width:60%; font-size:12px;'")

# 4. ROC CURVE (Row Plot 1)
roc_data <- all_preds %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Model) %>%
  yardstick::roc_curve(truth = resp_bin, prob_Refractory, event_level = "second")

# p_roc <- ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, color = Model)) +
#   geom_abline(lty = 3, color = "grey70") +
#   geom_path(linewidth = 1.2) +
#   scale_color_manual(values = nature_cols) +
#   labs(title = "Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) Curves",
#        x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)", 
#        y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)") +
#   coord_equal() + 
#   theme_nature()

# 5. PR CURVE (Row Plot 2)
pr_data <- all_preds %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Model) %>%
  yardstick::pr_curve(truth = resp_bin, prob_Refractory, event_level = "second")

# p_pr <- ggplot(pr_data, aes(x = recall, y = precision, color = Model)) +
#   geom_path(linewidth = 1.2) +
#   scale_color_manual(values = nature_cols) +
#   labs(title = "Precision-Recall Curves",
#        x = "Recall (Sensitivity)", 
#        y = "Precision") +
#   theme_nature()
# 
# # 6. SEQUENTIAL OUTPUT
# # Plotting them independently as row plots
# print(p_roc)
# print(p_pr)
```



```{r figure_1_model_metrics_barplot, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.width=8.5, fig.height=5}

library(scales)

# Wide table format:
# Metric | Model 1 (Clin + Gen) | Model 2 (+Transcripts) | Model 3 (+RTEs)

metrics_long <- metrics_df %>%
  dplyr::rename(Metric = 1) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -Metric,
    names_to = "Model",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = metrics_df[[1]]),
    Model  = factor(Model, levels = names(nature_cols))
  )

p_metrics <- ggplot(metrics_long, aes(x = Metric, y = Value, fill = Model)) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
    width = 0.72,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", Value)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = nature_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1.05),
    labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Model Performance Summary",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Metric value"
  ) +
  theme_nature() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(p_metrics)



```
